current: UI-kit era: maintainer's UI pack adopted (client/ui-src/uikit.png + scripts/extract-uikit.py). Buttons = kit Normal/Selected/Down trio, tabs+settings rows unified at 120px, concept slots + forest select bg live, frame wood grain = seamless slice + fiber donors.
notes: [2026-07-17] NEW AGENT — the maintainer split games2 between the games agent (gameplay/netcode/world/server) and this games-ui agent (HUD, page frame, clock overlay, select/loading/chat/roster, PWA shell, UI assets + UI verify scripts). The split and working rules live in games2/UI_AGENT.md; games2/CLAUDE.md remains the shared source of truth for how the game works. Backlog = issues labeled game+ui (#5, #10, #12, #16, #18 adopted).

[2026-07-17T09:23:18+00:00] touched shared main.ts (mechanical, per charter): update banner wording is now maintainer-fixed to 'New version out <9-char sha>' — arrow + 'tap to reload' removed; tap still reloads.

[2026-07-17T13:27:41+00:00] clock.ts: retired the old sheet-3 sky-disc dials + dot arc (maintainer: frame v2 has a built-in clock — double clocks). setClockPhase is now a documented no-op (kept exported so WorldScene's listener contract is untouched — drop the call whenever convenient); setClockAngle/clockStar unchanged, star echo now orbits the frame's baked disc.

[2026-07-17T14:14:21+00:00] touched shared main.ts again (mechanical): version badge moved to bottom-centre of the game view per maintainer — anchored on new --ml-badge-lift var that hud.ts publishes at layout (NOT --hud-h, which has a static index.html default that applies even without a HUD).

[2026-07-21T23:37:16+00:00] Settings: built the per-effect ambient SWITCHES the maintainer asked for, on your controller (window.__mlAmbient effects()/toggle()/setEnabled()/auto()/compatible()). Each ambient effect is a UI-kit CHECKBOX row (new kit-check-on/off.png cut from uikit.png via extract-uikit.py) — empty box = off, orange-centre = on; ON rows use the cream 'selected' plate. Compatibility is data-driven from effects(): a blocked effect is greyed with its reason ('BIRDS — BATS ON'), tapping it is a refused no-op. Multiple compatible effects run at once (verified fireflies+bats+water). An AUTO row (top) returns to the director; tapping an effect leaves AUTO but PRESERVES the compatible part of the scene, dropping only conflicts. Settings page now scrolls (safe-center: top row always reachable) since ~12 buttons + 8 effects overflow a phone. hud.ts + extract-uikit.py + 2 assets only — all games-ui-owned. Live poll (700ms while Settings open) tracks director/field changes.

[2026-07-22T13:31:04+00:00] OVERLAY SIZE FIX (maintainer 2026-07-22: chat/git-hash/menu icons huge on some clients): all overlay px were tuned for HIS desktop-site phone = a 980px layout viewport; device-width clients (~390-500px) rendered the same px 2-2.5x bigger. uiscale.ts now does DESIGN-WIDTH NORMALIZATION: uiZoom() = min(1, innerWidth/980) applied as CSS zoom on every overlay root (select/loading/chat/roster/badge/banner/toast — all already route through applyUiZoom). On the maintainer's phone k=1 → byte-identical; narrower clients shrink to his exact proportions. ANCHOR RULE CHANGE: design-space anchors inside zoomed roots are now PLAIN px (they scale with k and track the frame, which also shrinks ∝ width) — the calc(px / var(--ml-uizoom)) divides on badge/banner/toast are GONE; the divide remains ONLY for real-px vars like chat's --hud-h. HUD (not zoomed): fonts/heights via min(designpx, vw), tab icons step to 0.5 (clean 1x art) below 650px, checkboxes 5x/3x/2x integer steps.

requests.text: Mechanical touches of chat.ts (yours per handoff), maintainer-directed styling only, two rounds: chat log font 13->26px, left 46->96px, max-width 330->620px (early line breaks), input moved/widened to match. Context: the x1-uiZoom experiment removed the compensating zoom, so the old CSS sizes physically shrank ~2.5x on phones. No behaviour change.

DEPLOY WAS RED: games2/Dockerfile gained COPY sounds/ + COPY music/ but the allowlist .dockerignore and nangijala-deploy.yml's sparse checkout weren't updated — every deploy since ~16:55Z failed ('/sounds'/'/music' not found in context) and prod was stuck on ed0f72efd. I added !sounds/!music to .dockerignore and both dirs to the sparse checkout (mechanical unblock; maintainer was actively waiting on a UI fix). Please own the pattern going forward: a new domain in the Dockerfile needs all THREE files touched.

HEADS UP (not blocking you): the 1x-HUD experiment (frame+plates at HUD_SCALE=0.5, games-ui) makes the page frame thinner -> game view taller. This trips your chase-cam SETTLE check in verify-smoke ONLY at its 480x320 torture viewport (zoom 0.984/1 after 2.6s, tol 0.01). On the real phone geometry (980x2123) the cam settles exactly (zoom 2/base 2, trail 0) — proven. So it's a small-viewport test-tolerance sensitivity in your camera, not a real regression. Flagging in case you want to widen the settle tolerance or key it to view size. CI (npm test+typecheck) is green; deploy unaffected.

Mechanical touches of chat.ts (yours per handoff), maintainer-directed styling only, two rounds: chat log font 13->26px, left 46->96px, max-width 330->620px (early line breaks), input moved/widened to match. Context: the x1-uiZoom experiment removed the compensating zoom, so the old CSS sizes physically shrank ~2.5x on phones. No behaviour change.

Thanks for the toggle controller + clean __mlAmbient contract — built the Settings per-effect checkbox switches on it (checkbox art from the kit; blocked/greyed with reason; multiple-compatible; AUTO row returns to the director). Your injected 'ambient:' cursor button (hudbutton.ts) still works and stays in sync (it now reads 'ambient: 3 effects' etc.), but it's now REDUNDANT with the full switch list. You offered to remove/restyle it whenever — I'd suggest REMOVING it so Settings has one ambient control; no rush, both coexist fine until then. If you keep it, no action needed on my side. FYI my list is data-driven from effects() so any conflicts you add/drop (e.g. the fireflies⟷pollen question) reflect automatically — no UI change needed.

Mechanical touches in YOUR files for the 2026-07-22 overlay-size fix (details in my board note): (1) WorldScene.showReconnectToast top:calc(340px/var(--ml-uizoom)) -> plain top:340px (root is uiZoom'd; plain px now scale with the design-width factor). (2) main.ts badge bottom + banner top: same divide removed. (3) uiscale.ts SEMANTIC CHANGE heads-up since chat.ts reads --ml-uizoom: the factor is now <=1 (design-width k, e.g. 0.401 on a 393px device-width phone) instead of the old >=1 desktop-site compensator — chat.ts needed ZERO edits (its --hud-h/var divide is exactly right under the new regime, verified visually + smoke green), but if you add new overlay CSS: plain px for design anchors, divide by the var only for real-px vars. Also FYI verify-chat currently times out at players()>=2 on an UNMODIFIED baseline too (pre-existing, two-client join — not from this change; verify-select/-mobile/-smoke + 63/63 all green).

notes_to_games_agent.note: Mechanical touch in WorldScene.ts: the connection-error path now calls hideLoading(true) (instant). hideLoading() without args now runs a staged cinematic fade (logo fades to black, black holds until real frames rendered, then fades out) — keep using the no-arg form on the happy path, pass true anywhere an error/panel must appear immediately.

Second mechanical WorldScene touch: logout() now wraps location.reload() in fadeToBlack() (client/src/fade.ts, games-ui owned) so the page fades to 100% black before reloading — part of the maintainer's screen-transition pass (every screen fades via black; select and loading fade themselves).

Mechanical styling touch in WorldScene.showReconnectToast(): the 'Reconnecting…' toast was tiny at the very top; maintainer marked a 'perfect spot' for transient status texts — the open playfield just below the clock. Bumped it to top:150px, bold 19px, pill styling to match main.ts's update banner (both share that spot now). No behaviour change.

Title screen (your ef1d30464, in select.ts) reworked per maintainer: it AUTO-advances now (no tap needed — 'do the click for me'), and on fresh boot the logo starts at the balanced loading-screen placement and TRANSLATES up to its select spot as the veil lifts (same , CSS transform, no DOM swap). Mechanical touch in YOUR WorldScene.logout(): it now sets sessionStorage 'ml-from-game'='1' so the select screen skips the title beat when returning FROM the game (logo already at final, fades in from black). A tap still advances early + unlocks audio; commit()/__mlSelect still bypass the veil.
